attrs.alt: Diagram for The Context Editing Tool

content.text: automatically removes stale tool calls and results from the context window
when approaching token limits. Unlike manual compaction (which summarizes conversation history), context editing

: Summarizes all previous messages → lossy compression
-
: Removes only stale tool uses/results → precise deletion
(like your distributed systems engineering), tool accumulation is the biggest context killer:

Turn 1: [read_file: auth.ts] → 8,000 tokens
Turn 2: [grep: "error"] → 1,200 tokens  
Turn 3: [test_run: auth.test.ts] → 4,500 tokens
Turn 4: [read_file: guard.ts] → 7,200 tokens
...
Total: 100+ tool results → 180k tokens

:
- Agents hit context limits → workflows fail
- Token consumption explodes → higher costs
- Model focuses on stale data → degraded performance
: Complete workflows that would otherwise fail,

1. Context grows → approach token limit
2. Context editing detects stale tool uses
3. Removes old tool calls + results (not user/assistant)
4. Preserves conversation flow
5. Agent continues without interruption

(older calls)
- Tool results (outputs)
- Thinking blocks (previous reasoning)

:
- User messages
- Assistant responses
- Conversation structure
Removes old tool results when 2 new ones accumulate, keeps most recent.

: 39% performance improvement vs. 29% for editing alone.
